title: Sabbat Crusade
notes: Collection/anthology row present; individual contents not explicitly recoverable from TLBranson list.

curation.synopsis: The second Sabbat Worlds anthology, edited by Dan Abnett and published by Black Library in 2014, expands the Crusade-anthology format with new fiction from Dan Abnett, Aaron Dembski-Bowden, Nick Kyme, Nik Vincent, John French, Rob Sanders, Matthew Farrer and David Annandale. Abnett contributes four Tanith First pieces — 'Family', 'You Never Know', 'Ghosts and Bad Shadows' and 'A Ghost Return' — that fill in regimental gaps between Salvation's Reach and the Victory arc. John French's 'Son of Sek' centres the rising Anarch infantry tier as a strategic threat; Aaron Dembski-Bowden's 'Arnogaur' explores the Blood Pact officer corps from the inside; Nick Kyme's 'Tempest' returns to the Phantine theatre; Nik Vincent's 'Viduity' and David Annandale's 'The Deeper Wounds' run smaller-scale Crusade-civilian and trooper-level threads. The anthology sits chronologically across the late-Saint and early-Victory arcs, with the Sirkle-style internal-Imperium conspiracy and the Sek/Innokenti Archenemy split as recurring framing concerns.
